parsed_review.summary: CP00-905 is a descriptor-only HRX bridge continuing the RP30 P01 CompensationRecord tail (2 rows) into the RP30 P02 RuleEngine foundation (8 rows) across RP30.P01.M10.S04-RP30.P02.M00.S08. Read-only inspection confirms the pack is internally consistent and does not over-claim. HRX_CP905_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:219) matches the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the review baseline, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json packs[] entry exactly (pack CP00-905, risk A, 10 units, range and unit IDs, upstream CP00-904, next CP00-906 / RP30.P02.M00.S09); all included-unit titles match 1:1. Implementation (registry.js:1881-2053) builds deepFrozen descriptor rows with every runtime/write/real-data/secret flag forced false and compensation amounts, currency, payroll runtime, rule definitions, rule execution, decision writes, and HR AI final judgment all closed. Validators (validators.js:1320-1512) exhaustively check all 10 rows, deliverable/phase/micro/title distributions, authority boundary, Hermes/Claude packets, no-leak guards, and contract projection drift; 3 CP905 tests (model.test.js:457-521) assert zero errors and are wired into npm test. contracts/hrx-people-contract.json is in sync (current/latest CP00-905, projection HrxCp905CompensationRuleEngineBridgeDescriptor) and the rp30:hrx:validate script enforces the CP897->CP905 binding chain. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read-only and not final approver, human approval is required for runtime opening (deferred to CP00-906), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and the Hermes packet emits no runtime receipt (evidence-only). Manifest honestly reports production_ready false, runtime_ready false, status in_progress. Review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded with valid JSON/non-zero stdout/exit 0, prompt is within size with no embedded full diff/source, only Read/Grep/Glob are used, and no invalid review attempts exist for this pack (the LDIP invalid-attempt file is correctly segregated as unrelated dirty). No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 informational note: the pack's command-evidence.json records only the 6 HRX commands at exit 0 and is marked pending, so the full 10-command closeout ladder and adjudication normalization remain post-review steps that I cannot execute as a read-only reviewer; this matches the pack's own documented lifecycle and is not a defect, but production_ready promotion must follow that ladder passing.

findings.title: Full validation ladder and adjudication normalization remain post-review steps (pack-documented, expected lifecycle)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only the 6 HRX-specific commands (node --check, node --test, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) at exit_code 0, with status 'pending' and gate_outcome 'pending_pack_evidence_creation'. The 10-command closeout ladder listed in the review prompt (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet evidenced as passing in this pack, and adjudication.md / construction-inspection.json are both PENDING with production_ready false. As a read-only reviewer I confirmed descriptor, validator, test, and contract correctness by inspection but cannot execute the ladder. The pack itself documents this sequence (note: 'Full validation ladder must be re-run after review normalization, adjudication update, and queue regeneration'), so this is informational, not a defect, and the pack is not over-claiming (production_ready is false).
findings.recommendation: During closeout, run and record the full 10-command validation ladder at exit 0, normalize adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json to this review outcome, ensure git diff --check is clean, and only then promote production_ready. No change to descriptor/code is required.
